data.title: Technical View: July series begins on positive note; Nifty must break 25,800 for bull run as India VIX hits 9-month low
data.description:
Nifty Uptrend

The Nifty 50 kicked off the July series on a positive note, closing at a fresh nine-month high after the India VIX dropped to its lowest level in nine months on June 27. Healthy FII buying in the previous session also lifted market sentiment. The index extended its upward journey for the third consecutive session and continued forming higher lows for the sixth straight day, closing well above the upper Bollinger Band, accompanied by a noticeable expansion in the bands.

The index now needs to close and sustain above the 25,750–25,800 zone — the upper band of the gap-down area from October 3, 2024 — to continue its upward momentum. A decisive breakout above this level could open the door to 26,000, followed by a possible move toward the record high of 26,277. On the downside, the 25,300–25,400 zone remains a crucial support area, according to experts.

After an initial hour of volatility, the Nifty 50 gained strength and extended its upward move, hitting an intraday high of 25,654.20 in afternoon trade. It eventually closed at 25,638, up 89 points, marking the highest closing level since October 1, 2024. The index formed a bullish candle on the daily chart, supported by above-average volumes for another session.

Momentum indicators such as the RSI, Stochastic RSI, and MACD maintained their positive crossovers, with further bullish bias visible in the MACD histogram.

On the weekly chart, the Nifty formed a strong bullish candle after five weeks of rangebound action, gaining 2.09 percent. This is seen as a positive signal and suggests more upside in the near term.

According to Nagaraj Shetti, Senior Technical Research Analyst at HDFC Securities, the underlying trend for the Nifty remains positive. “The next upside to be watched is around the 25,800–26,000 levels by next week. Immediate support is placed at 25,400,” he said.

Weekly options data indicate that 26,000 is likely to act as the next resistance for the Nifty 50, with support at 25,500. The maximum Call open interest was observed at the 26,000 strike, followed by 26,500 and 25,900. Maximum Call writing was seen at the 25,900, 26,000, and 25,700 strikes. On the Put side, the 25,500 strike held the highest open interest, followed by 25,000 and 25,400, with maximum Put writing at the 25,600, 25,500, and 25,000 strikes.

Bank Nifty

The banking benchmark Bank Nifty also continued its upward rally, rising 237 points to end at a new closing high of 57,444, forming a bullish candle with a lower shadow on the daily timeframe. The index closed above the upper Bollinger Band with above-average volumes. On the weekly timeframe, it formed a strong bullish candle with 2.12 percent gains, signaling a healthy trend ahead.

According to Chandan Taparia, Head – Technical Research and Derivatives at Motilal Oswal Financial Services, “The index now needs to hold above the 57,250 zone for an up move towards new life highs around 58,000 and then 58,500 levels. On the downside, support is seen at 57,250, followed by 57,000 zones.”

Meanwhile, the India VIX, which measures expected market volatility, extended its downward move for the fourth consecutive session, falling 1.61 percent to 12.39 — its lowest closing level since October 1, 2024 — placing the bulls in a comfort zone.

data.title: HDB Financial Services IPO becomes most subscribed billion-dollar Indian IPO in 4 years
data.description:
HDB Financial Services IPO

HDB Financial Services Ltd.'s $1.5 billion initial public offering is set to become India's most sought after large listing in at least four years on heavy bidding from foreign and domestic investors.

The offering from the shadow-lending unit of the country's top private bank got bids for a little over two billion shares as of Friday afternoon in Mumbai, versus the 130.4 million shares available, according to data on the BSE's website. Global investors, domestic mutual funds and financial institutions made up the bulk of the demand.

Individual investors who bid for more than 1 million rupees of shares were also among the biggest backers of the stock. The portion reserved for small investors was also fully subscribed.

The over 15 times-demand for HDB shares is the biggest since food delivery firm Eternal Ltd.’s $1.4 billion IPO was subscribed more than 29 times, exchange data showed.

Also read: HDB Financial IPO closes with 17x subscription; QIB portion booked 55x

The share sale comes amid a rebound in the benchmark NSE Nifty 50 Index, and may signal strong appetite for large Indian issuances ahead. The index is now less than 5% shy of its peak in September.

There are some potentially bigger deals expected later this year, including those of Tata Capital Ltd. and LG Electronics Inc.’s domestic unit.

India emerged among the world’s busiest markets for capital raising in 2024. After a lull, block trades, institutional share placements and IPOs have picked up.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

HDB Financial raised 33.7 billion rupees by allocating shares to anchor investors including Life Insurance Corp. of India, the country’s biggest insurer, and domestic mutual funds. Funds managed by Morgan Stanley and Allianz SE were among the global investors.

Follow all IPO news here.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: NBFCs speeding up IPO plans, likely to raise over Rs 30,000 crore in FY26: Report
data.description:
IPO

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Non banking financial companies are expected to speed up their initial public offering (IPO) processes to benefit from easier regulatory norm, lower interest rates, increasingly positive market sentiment and more, The Economic Times reported.

Several NBFCs have already received approval from market regulator SEBI. These companies are planning to speed up their listing plans while looking for better market opportunities, the report added.

Vehicle and business loan company SK Finance, education loan financers Avanse Financial Services and Credila Financial Services, and MSME loan company Veritas Finance will likely raise a total of Rs 13,500 crore, the report cited bankers as saying. Tata Capital meanwhile is working on an IPO to raise over Rs 17,000 crore.

Moneycontrol couldn't independently verify the report.

Tata Capital has reportedly received SEBI's nod for its IPO, which is expected to be India's biggest listing this year. The share sale is likely to be launched as early as August this year.

The conditions are currently more favourable for IPO-bound NBFCs because of the change in stance, ample liquidity in the banking system and a favourable rate cycle, the report cited ICICI Securities Executive Director Ajay Saraf as saying. Notably, Saraf is one of the bankers appointed to manage the maiden public issues of Tata Capital and SK Finance.

RBI's recent move to cut its repo rate by 50 basis points and lower cash reserve ratio (CRR) by 100 bps will help the NBFCs address any funding challenge they faced earlier, the report added.

The worst of the NBFC credit cycle is behind us, the report quoted Shreepal Doshi, lead analyst on NBFCs at Equirus Securities, as saying. Doshi added that the elevated credit costs which were seen in 2024 are now easing, hence it is fair to expect some capital raising by the NBFCs in the second and third quarter onwards.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Notably, the 12,500-crore IPO of HDFC Bank's subsidiary HDB Financial Services is set to close today for public bidding. The IPO, which the largest by an NBFC in India, comprises a fresh issue of Rs 2,500 crore, and an offer for sale (OFS) of Rs 10,000 crore by parent company HDFC Bank, which holds 94.3 percent stake in the NBFC.

data.title: How JSW plans to fund acquisition of Akzo Nobel's India unit
data.description:
JSW Group agreed to buy up to 75% of Akzo Nobel NV’s Indian business in a deal valued at about $1.6 billion, including debt

JSW Group is in talks with global lenders to raise about Rs 4,000 crore ($468 million) to partly fund its purchase of Akzo Nobel NV’s local business, according to people familiar with the matter.

Foreign banks including Barclays Plc, Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group Inc. and Standard Chartered Bank Plc will arrange the debt, which is expected to be denominated in rupees, said the people, who asked not to be identified because the information is confidential.

JSW Group has also reached out to international private credit funds including Ares Management Corp, Cerberus Capital Management and Farallon Capital Management though nothing has been finalized yet, said the people. Morgan Stanley, a bank on the acquistion, will also be involved in the borrowing, two of the people said.

JSW Group agreed to buy up to 75% of Akzo Nobel NV’s Indian business in a deal valued at about $1.6 billion, including debt, as the Indian conglomerate seeks to expand its presence in areas such as paints.

A rupee bond deal would be a boon for supply in India’s booming local debt market, which year to date has seen issuance of 6.5 trillion rupees ($76 billion), up 28.4% from a year ago, according to data compiled by Bloomberg.

Acquisition financing has been a bright spot for global lenders in India, particularly private credit funds. In the second half of 2024, private credit deals showed a “strong focus” on such deals, global consulting firm Ernst & Young wrote in a report.

The planned debt could be raised in multiple tranches and levels, and the tenor could be up to three years, said the people. The terms and currency of the deal are not yet final and could still change, they added.

Ares and Standard Chartered declined to comment. JSW Group, Barclays, Mitsubishi and other lenders did not immediately respond to requests for comment.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Taking Stock: Markets rise for a fourth day to take Nifty past 25,600, Nifty Bank hits record high
data.description:
Market Today

Benchmark indices ended in the green for a fourth straight session on June 27, with Nifty 50 managing to touch a high of 25,650, powered by broad-based buying on hopes of extension of Trump tariff deadline and the possibility of a soon-than-expected US Fed rate cut.

The Nifty Bank index hit another record high in the session to end above 57,400 for the first time.

At close, the Sensex was up 303.03 points or 0.36 percent at 84,058.90, and the Nifty was up 88.80 points or 0.35 percent at 25,637.80. BSE Midcap index rose 0.4 percent and smallcap index gained 0.5 percent.

For the week, BSE Sensex and Nifty added 2 percent each.

Read More: SBI likely to raise Rs 5,000 crore through Tier-II bonds by August, says report

Stocks & Sectors in Action

Among sectors, except consumer durables, IT and realty, all other indices ended in the green with Capital Goods, Healthcare, oil & gas, power, telecom, PSU Bank up 0.5-1 percent.

Jio Financial, IndusInd Bank, Adani Enterprises, Asian Paints, Apollo Hospitals were among major gainers on the Nifty, while losers were Trent, Dr Reddy's Labs, Eternal, Wipro, Tata Consumer.

Also Read: IndusInd Bank shares surge on report that Axis Bank's Rajiv Anand likely strong candidate for CEO role

Akzo Nobel India shares rose 6% after JSW Paints said it will acquire stake worth Rs 8,986 crore, Jio Financial shares gained 4 percent after SEBI clears Jio BlackRock Broking to operate as stockbroker, SBI shares rose 1% as CLSA maintains outperform call, Torrent Pharma shares surged 4% after JPMorgan upgraded it to overweight, and Ahluwalia Contracts' shares added 5 percent on winning two contracts.

More than 130 stocks on the BSE touched their 52-week highs, including Godfrey Phillips, Navin Fluorine, Nuvama Wealth, Abbott India, Solar Industries, Interglobe Aviation, Laurus Labs, Max Healthcare, Dalmia Bharat, TVS Motor, HDFC Life, MCX India, Bharti Airtel, Gillette India, Hyundai Motor, Max Financial, Fortis Healthcare, Lloyds Metals, among others. Click to View More

Global Markets

Earlier, Wall Street ended higher on Thursday, nudging S&P 500 and the Nasdaq nearer to record highs after the Israel-Iran ceasefire eased geopolitical concerns and a raft of economic indicators appeared to support the case for a US Fed rate cut this year. Asian shares hit their highest level in more than three years, tracking the Wall Street rally.

Outlook for June 30

Rupak De, Senior Technical Analyst at LKP Securities

The Nifty continued to move higher as investor confidence remained strong. With no major resistance seen before 25,750–25,800, the index may continue its upward trajectory. However, the rally might not be sharp, and it could take time to reach the 25,800 mark. A buy-on-dips strategy appears more appropriate at current levels, following the sharp rise over the past few days. On the downside, support is placed at 25,500; a break below this level could lead to consolidation.

Ajit Mishra – SVP, Research, Religare Broking

Markets edged higher on Friday, extending the ongoing uptrend and ending the session with modest gains. After a flat start, the Nifty gradually moved up during the first half, followed by a range-bound phase until the close. It eventually settled near the day’s high at 25,637.80.

On the sectoral front, most indices contributed to the up move, with energy and pharma emerging as the top gainers. The broader indices also maintained their positive momentum, posting gains in the range of 0.3% to 0.86%.

The recent geopolitical stability has improved risk sentiment, as seen in the broad-based market participation. Moreover, positive developments around potential trade agreements could further strengthen the bullish bias. We continue to recommend a “buy on dips” strategy on the index, with an emphasis on selective stock picking for better opportunities.

data.title: Sambhv Steel Tubes IPO gets 28 times subscription on day 3 of bidding - check latest GMP
data.description:
Sambhv Steel Tubes IPO continues to garner robust subscription. 

Sambhv Steel Tubes' initial public offer (IPO) received over 28 times subscription on the day 3 of bidding on Friday. The initial share sale fetched bids for 138 crore shares against 4.92 crore shares on offer, according to data available with the NSE.

Non-institutional investor's portion subscribed 31.63 times, while the quota for retail individual investors (RIIs) garnered 7.29 times subscription. Qualified institutional buyers (QIBs) part received 62.32 times subscription.

data.title: Gainers & Losers: Top 10 stocks that moved the most on June 27
data.description:
Top gainers and losers

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Benchmark indices Nifty and Sensex recorded a higher close for a fourth session on June 27, with Nifty making a nine-month high and Nifty Bank closing at all-time high.

Sensex closed 303.03 points or 0.36 percent higher at 84,058.90, while Nifty ended the session 88.80 points or 0.35 percent higher at 25,637.80. Easing geopolitical tensions following the Israel-Iran ceasefire and optimism around a potential US-India trade deal have been key tailwinds for the rally.

Here are the stocks that moved the most on June 27:

IndusInd Bank

IndusInd Bank shares sharply surged nearly 4 percent in trade on June 27, after CNBC-TV18 reported that Axis Bank's Rajiv Anand is a strong candidate for the CEO position. HDFC Bank's Rahul Shukla and Bajaj Finance's Anup Saha are also likely to be running for the role, the channel said, citing sources.

Jio Financial Services

Jio Financial Services shares rose over 4 percent intraday on Friday after the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) approved Jio BlackRock Broking to function as a stockbroker.

Adani Enterprises

Adani Enterprises shares closed over 2 percent higher, tracking gains in metal shares after White House Press Secretary said the July 9 deadline for reimposition of tariff on imports was 'not critical' and could be extended, but it is for the President to decide.

Varun Beverages

Varun Beverages shares rose 2 percent in trade, snapping a two-day losing streak after HSBC retained its buy rating and said the recent correction in the stock has been unwarranted. With a price target of Rs 670 per share, the international brokerage implies an upside potential of over 46 percent from the last closing price of Rs 458 per share on the NSE.

State Bank of India (SBI)

Shares of State Bank of India gained over a percent on June 27 after CLSA maintained an outperform rating on the lender, citing the bank's strong positioning in the coming quarters. With a target price of Rs 1,050 per share, the brokerage implies an upside potential of 32 percent from the last close of Rs 797 per share on the NSE. SBI has continued to gain market share for the fourth consecutive year, not only from PSU peers but also from some private sector banks. While deposit growth was modest, its CASA performance outpaced that of peers, CLSA said.

Torrent Pharma

Torrent Pharma shares rose 4 percent in trade after global brokerage JPMorgan upgraded the stock to ‘Overweight’, citing strong growth levers in the coming quarters. With a target price of Rs 3,800, JPMorgan implies an upside potential of 18 percent from the last closing price. The previous target price given by the brokerage was Rs 3,650.

Akzo Nobel India

Shares of paints major Akzo Nobel India rallied over six percent in early trade after JSW Paints agreed to acquire up to 74.76 percent stake in the company. JSW Paints signed agreements to acquire up to 74.76 percent stake in Akzo Nobel India from its parent company and affiliates for up to Rs 8,986 crore, subject to final adjustments, the company said in a filing. The deal will need approval from the Competition Commission of India and includes a mandatory open offer to public shareholders.

Prime Focus

Shares of Prime Focus rose sharply on Friday, rising as much as 20 percent in trade amid strong volumes, marking the third consecutive session of gains. In a large block deal during opening trade, around 15.1 million shares of Prime Focus were exchanged, contributing to the spike in volume.

Zudio-parent Trent

Zudio-parent Trent shares fell over a percent as investors may have resorted to profit booking at elevated levels in the stock that is trading at a P/E of 135x.

Axis Bank

Axis Bank shares closed in the red after a significant rise the previous day. However, the Nifty Bank index had hit a fresh lifetime high today, breaking its previous day's record.

data.title: Prime Focus shares rise up to 20% intraday amid high volume
data.description:
Prime Focus shares rise up to 20% intraday amid high volume.

Shares of Prime Focus rose sharply on Friday, gaining as much as 20 percent in intraday trade on the NSE amid strong volumes.

The stock touched a high of Rs 136.83 during the day, after opening with a gain of 6.69 percent. It continued to rise through the session.

This marks the third consecutive session of gains for the company’s shares.

In a large block deal during opening trade, around 15.1 million shares of Prime Focus were exchanged, contributing to the spike in volume.

data.title: SBI likely to raise Rs 5,000 crore through Tier-II bonds by August, says report
data.description:
State Bank of India (SBI)

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

India's largest lender State Bank of India (SBI) is likely to raise funds worth nearly Rs 5,000 crore through debt instruments over the next two months, news agency Reuters reported on June 27 citing people familiar with the matter, helping the shares sustain early gains of nearly a percent on a day the Bank Nifty extended its rally to record highs.

The PSU lender is planning to raise funds through the issuance of Basel III-compliant tier II bonds with a 10-year or 15-year maturity, in July or August, the report said citing sources, with initial talks having begun and the duration of the bond issuance would be finalized considering the rate at the time of issuance, the report added.

One source told the news agency that the 15-year bond structure could be explored with a call option at the end of 10 years.

Moneycontrol couldn't independently verify the report.

Earlier in May, SBI had approved the proposal to raise up to $3 billion as long-term fund in single or multiple tranches during the ongoing FY26 through a public offer, or a private placement of senior unsecured notes in US Dollar or any other major foreign currency.

SBI Chairman CS Setty had said during the fourth quarter earnings call that the uncertainty over tariffs will impact the economy and the investment scenario. "Without raising capital, the bank's growth will not be impacted. Benign asset quality is likely to continue for more period," SBI Chairman CS Setty had said.

The public lender had earlier raised Rs 7,500 in September last year through 15-year tier II bonds, which had a call option at the end of 10 years. In the previous financial year (FY25), the bank had raised 150 billion rupees through tier II bonds.

Also Read: Nifty Bank hits new all-time high again, tops 57,400 for the first time ever

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

On June 25, Bloomberg News had reported citing people familiar with the development that State Bank of India had selected investment bankers including Citigroup and HSBC to manage the share sale. SBI has also chosen ICICI Securities, Kotak Capital, Morgan Stanley and SBI Capital for the fundraise, the report added.

data.title: NSE 500 companies’ margins near historical peak, profit growth slow: Experts fear liquidity alone may not be enough to sustain rally
data.description:
According to a recent report by DSP Mutual Fund, the current median EBITDA margin for NSE 500 companies (excluding BFSI) stands at 17.6% which is already at the higher end of the historical range.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Corporate India’s profitability is currently hovering near decade-high levels, and with scope of margin expansion muted, some experts believe sustaining this momentum may prove challenging.

With topline growth of NSE 500 companies hovering in the low single-digits, margin expansion remains the primary driver of PAT growth. According to a recent report by DSP Mutual Fund, the current median EBITDA margin for these companies (excluding BFSI) stands at 17.6%, which is already at the higher end of the historical range.

“Since the post-COVID recovery, margins have played a pivotal role in driving profitability. This improvement has largely been the result of enhanced cost efficiencies, balance sheet restructuring (reflected in reduced interest and credit costs). However, rising crude oil prices now pose a risk to margin stability. With limited room left for further margin expansion and muted topline growth, the sustainability of profit growth is increasingly uncertain. This could challenge current valuations and the growth expectations priced into many stocks,” the DSP MF report said.

Some experts suggest that without a pickup in private investment or a revival in global growth, particularly in services exports, the current environment offers limited upside. Structural constraints in consumption and the absence of new export catalysts mean the outlook remains cautious.

What the Numbers Show

Most sectors are currently operating close to their peak profit margins for FY25, according to data from DSP Mutual Fund. “These elevated levels across most sectors suggest that margin expansion from here could be challenging,” the report said.

Explaining the rationale, Pragati Aggarwal, DSP Mutual Fund’s Product Manager and author of the note said the revenue growth for Indian companies has slowed to low to mid-single digits. While corporate India is currently enjoying strong free cash flows, there’s a clear reluctance to step up capital expenditure, or capex. After years of government-led spending, it was expected that private corporates would take over the investment baton in the post-pandemic phase, but that hasn’t materialized.

“The topline boost many companies were seeing has also started to taper off. While some sectoral opportunities remain like private banks and healthcare, most of the market appears to be at or near its margin peak. This leaves little headroom for further expansion,” Aggarwal added.

Aggarwal said that India’s GDP growth, even at 6.5 percent, is below its potential. While the RBI is doing its part through monetary measures, further acceleration requires fiscal support, which the government has already extended extensively. “With limited room to take on more debt, the onus is now on the private sector to invest. However, corporates are not showing signs of stepping up, citing a lack of compelling opportunities.”

A recent Nuvama report has concurred with this assessment, fearing that India’s bull market may be ageing, with sectoral leadership driven by short-lived themes rather than a decade-long structural trend. "Overall, the 2020s bull market is ageing. Liquidity improvement has lifted markets higher from their lows. However, the wedge between growth and valuations has substantially increased, capping the upside," Nuvama said.

Post-Covid profits were led by restructuring, but margins are now peaking, and demand remains weak. Valuations, it Nuvama said, are expensive across sectors despite slowing earnings, posing downside risks. “The BSE 500 median trailing P/E stands at 38x, with earnings growth of just 9%, resulting in a median PEG ratio of 4x - well above sustainable levels. This valuation disconnect along with fading policy tailwinds and shallow rate cuts suggests that the market may struggle to find new drivers for growth,” the Nuvama note added.

Policy support is limited due to fiscal consolidation, and with no clear macro theme ahead, Nuvama said a bottom-up strategy focused on margin mean-reversion and undervalued laggards is advisable. Stock selection should favour low-margin, low-valuation names with potential for cost-driven earnings recovery.

On opportunities ahead, DSP MF’s Pragati Aggarwal said that household leverage is already elevated, limiting the scope for a consumption-led push. “India’s strong GDP growth in the past, especially between 2010 and 2019, was underpinned by robust global growth and a surge in services exports. But now, services seem to have hit a saturation point, and global uncertainties are weighing on broader export prospects. With both consumption and exports plateauing, the economy lacks clear drivers for a fresh leg of growth unless private capex revives,” she said.

But not all market participants agree to this caution. HDFC Securities’ Deven Vakil said they expect India Inc’s topline to grow by around 5% in aggregate earnings, and EBITDA to increase by 10% in the next quarterly earnings season for the stocks under their coverage. “We do not believe that the margins have peaked for all sectors across the markets on an aggregate level. We expect the Oil and Gas, Metals, Chemicals, Telecom, Cement, Automobiles and Capital Goods sectors to show better margins this quarter,” Vakil said, adding that IT, pharma and healthcare, consumer, capital markets, power are likely to show the same or slightly lower margins.

For example, talking about the telecom sector, he explains that margin performance has been robust, supported by the positive impact of tariff hikes and lower fuel costs. Similarly, for Auto, EBITDA margins are expected to improve sequentially as raw material prices have softened, and the effects of price hikes will start to be reflected in the financials.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.
